message.content:
The change is complete. The `registerDeveloperTool` function in `src/System/Agents/ToolRegistration.hs` now includes an explicit mention of the `help` capability in the tool description.

**Summary of changes:**

Updated the `toolDescriptionText` in the `registerDeveloperTool` function from:
```haskell
toolDescriptionText = "Developer tools for writing and validating agents and tools: " <> box.toolboxDescription
```

To:
```haskell
toolDescriptionText =
    "Developer tools for writing and validating agents and tools. "
        <> "Use the 'help' capability to get detailed documentation for all available capabilities. "
        <> box.toolboxDescription
```

**Acceptance criteria met:**
- ✅ The tool description explicitly mentions the `help` capability
- ✅ The description explains that `help` returns documentation for all available capabilities
- ✅ The change compiles without errors
- ✅ All existing tests pass

This change improves discoverability by informing the LLM that the `help` capability provides comprehensive documentation for all activated capabilities, encouraging LLMs to use it proactively when they need to understand tool usage.

1100	registerDeveloperTool ::
1101	    DeveloperTools.Toolbox ->
1102	    Either String ToolRegistration
1103	registerDeveloperTool box =
1104	    let
1105	        tName = "developer_tools"
1106	        llmName = developer2LLMName box tName
1107	
1108	        -- Build parameter properties based on enabled capabilities
1109	        paramProps = buildDeveloperToolParams box
1110	
1111	        toolDescription =
1112	            ToolDescription
1113	                { toolDescriptionName = llmName
1114	                , toolDescriptionText =
1115	                    "Developer tools for writing and validating agents and tools. "
1116	                        <> "Use the 'help' capability to get detailed documentation for all available capabilities. "
1117	                        <> box.toolboxDescription
1118	                , toolDescriptionParamProperties = paramProps
1119	                }
1120	
1121	        -- Create the tool
1122	        tool' = developerTool box
1123	
1124	        -- Find function - matches on the LLM name
1125	        find :: ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool ToolCall)

properties.propertyDescription: Capability to execute: read-file-range, write-file-range, patch-file
For validate-tool: Path to tool script
For scaffold-agent: Template (openai, mistral, ollama)
For scaffold-tool: Language (bash, python, haskell)
For scaffold operations: Slug/name for the generated file
For scaffold operations, restore-file: Output file path or path to restore
For scaffold operations: Overwrite existing files
For show-spec: Spec name (bash-tools)
For restore-file: MD5 hash reference of the snapshot to restore from
For read-file-range, write-file-range, patch-file, restore-file: Path to the file

Line ranges for file operations. Range formats: 'N' (single line), 'N-M' (range), 'N+' (after line N), 'head' (prepend), 'tail' (append), 'whole' (entire file). IMPORTANT: To CREATE a new file, use ranges='head'. For read-file-range: Omit ranges to read entire file. For write-file-range: Use contentBlocks array with one block per range.
For write-file-range: Array of content blocks, where each block corresponds to one range. Use empty strings to delete lines.

For write-file-range, patch-file: Optional MD5 hash for optimistic locking. Operation fails if current file content doesn't match this hash. Use value from previous operation's beforeSnapshotRef or afterSnapshotRef.
For patch-file: Unified diff patch content to apply

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

1120	
1121	        -- Find function - matches on the LLM name
1122	        find :: ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool ToolCall)
1123	        find call =
1124	            if call.callToolName == getToolName llmName

1130	     in

1590	    run :: Tracer IO Trace -> ToolExecutionContext -> Aeson.Value -> IO (CallResult ())
1591	    run tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do
1592	        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "capability") v of
1593	            Just (Aeson.String cap) -> do
1594	                -- Check if this is the attach-file capability
1595	                if cap == "attach-file"
1596	                    then handleAttachFile tracer v
1597	                    else do
1598	                        -- Extract optional parameters for session introspection capabilities
1599	                        let mSessionId = case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "session_id") v of
1600	                                Just (Aeson.String sid) -> Just sid
1601	                                _ -> Nothing
1602	                        let mQuery = case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "query") v of
1603	                                Just (Aeson.String q) -> Just q
1604	                                _ -> Nothing
1605	
1606	                        -- Extract read-session parameters
1607	                        let mReadParams =
1608	                                if cap == "read-session"
1609	                                    then Just $ extractReadSessionParams v
1610	                                    else Nothing

1612	                        result <- SystemTools.executeQueryWithParams (Prod.contramap SystemToolsTrace tracer) box cap mSessionId mQuery mReadParams
1613	                        case result of
1614	                            Left err -> pure $ SystemToolError call err
1615	                            Right rsp -> pure $ SystemToolResult call rsp
1616	            _ -> pure $ SystemToolError call (SystemTools.SystemInfoError "Missing 'capability' parameter or invalid type")

1620	    -- Handle the attach-file capability
1621	    handleAttachFile :: Tracer IO Trace -> Aeson.Object -> IO (CallResult ())
1622	    handleAttachFile tracer params = do
1623	        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "filepath") params of
1624	            Just (Aeson.String filePath) -> do
1625	                result <- SystemTools.executeAttachFile (Prod.contramap SystemToolsTrace tracer) box (Text.unpack filePath)
1626	                case result of
1627	                    Left err -> pure $ SystemToolError call err
1628	                    Right attachResult -> do
1629	                        -- Convert AttachFileResult to MediaAttachment and return as BlobToolSuccess
1630	                        let mediaType = SystemTools.detectMediaType (Text.unpack filePath)
1631	                        case mediaType of
1632	                            Nothing -> pure $ SystemToolError call (SystemTools.SystemInfoError "Could not detect media type")
1633	                            Just mt -> do
1634	                                -- Decode base64 data back to raw bytes for BlobToolSuccess
1635	                                let base64Data = SystemTools.attachBase64Data attachResult
1636	                                case B64.decode (Text.encodeUtf8 base64Data) of
1637	                                    Left err ->

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{"capability": "write-file-range", "path": "src/System/Agents/ToolRegistration.hs", "ranges": "1669-1690", "contentBlocks": ["-- | Builder for a DeveloperToolbox-based tool.
developerTool :: DeveloperTools.Toolbox -> Tool ()
developerTool box =
    ToolBase.Tool
        { ToolBase.toolDef = DeveloperTool toolDesc
        , ToolBase.toolRun = run
        }
  where
    call = ()
    toolDesc =
        DeveloperTools.ToolDescription
            { DeveloperTools.toolDescriptionName = "developer_tools"
            , DeveloperTools.toolDescriptionDescription = box.toolboxDescription
            , DeveloperTools.toolDescriptionToolboxName = box.toolboxName
            }
    run :: Tracer IO Trace -> ToolExecutionContext -> Aeson.Value -> IO (CallResult ())
    run tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do
        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "capability") v of
            Just (Aeson.String cap) -> executeDeveloperCapability tracer box cap v
            _ -> pure $ DeveloperToolError call (DeveloperTools.ValidationError "Missing 'capability' parameter or invalid type")
    run _tracer _ctx _ = do
        pure $ DeveloperToolError call (DeveloperTools.ValidationError "Arguments must be a JSON object")"]}

1691	-- | Execute a developer tool capability
1692	executeDeveloperCapability :: Tracer IO Trace -> DeveloperTools.Toolbox -> Text -> Aeson.Object -> IO (CallResult ())
1693	executeDeveloperCapability tracer box cap params = case cap of
1694	    "validate-tool" -> do
1695	        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "tool_path") params of

1700	                    Right valResult -> pure $ DeveloperToolResult () valResult
1701	            _ -> pure $ DeveloperToolError () (DeveloperTools.ValidationError "Missing 'tool_path' parameter")
1702	    "scaffold-agent" -> do
1703	        let mTemplate = case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "template") params of
1704	                Just (Aeson.String t) -> t
1705	                _ -> "openai"

1707	                Just (Aeson.String s) -> s
1708	                _ -> "new-agent"
1709	        let mFilePath = case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "file_path") params of
